context: (a)  Felty syndrome (< 1%) =  rheumatoid arthritis (present for > 10 years) + splenomegaly + neutropenia Age:   40-70 years; F > M; rare in Blacks (eg, olecranon) without calcifications (DDx to gout) (e)  Cardiovascular involvement √  splenomegaly (1-5%) Cystic Rheumatoid Arthritis =  intraosseous cystic lesions as dominant feature Pathogenesis:   increased pressure in synovial space from joint effusion → decompresses through microfractures of weakened marginal cortex into subarticular bone ◊  Increase in size + extent of cysts correlates with increased level of activity + absence of synovial
